bonuses.leadin: Though some scholars argue that this work was a preparatory sketch for a never-finished painting, its blue wash background was likely intended to resemble architectural drawings. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this silverpoint-on-panel drawing by Jan van Eyck that depicts the title saint reading and holding a large quill pen in front of an elaborate architectural scene.
Jan van Eyck’s interest in architectural drawings is also evidenced by the Gothic building in the background of the “adoration of the lamb panel” of his altarpiece named for this city.

Reflectography has revealed that van Eyck removed a large coin purse that this man was wearing in the underdrawing of a painting that depicts him sitting in a loggia opposite the Virgin Mary being crowned by an angel.
answers: Chancellor Rolin [or Nicolas Rolin; accept Madonna of Chancellor Rolin; prompt on chancellor]
answers_sanitized: Ghent [or Gent; or Gand; accept Ghent Altarpiece]
Chancellor Rolin [or Nicolas Rolin; accept Madonna of Chancellor Rolin; prompt on chancellor]

Reflectography has revealed that van Eyck removed a large coin purse that this man was wearing in the underdrawing of a painting that depicts him sitting in a loggia opposite the Virgin Mary being crowned by an angel.

bonuses.leadin: This saint is often portrayed standing in a tower with three windows, making reference to how her father Dioscorus kept her in a tower. For 10 points each:
parts: Dioscorus was struck by lightning for beheading which saint, who thus became the patron saint of explosives and artillerymen, among other things?
Saint Barbara was venerated as one of the Fourteen Holy Helpers, another member of whom was this patron saint of England. He is well-known for slaying a dragon.

Another of the Fourteen Holy Helpers is this saint, who protected against sudden death. Since she consecrated her virginity to Jesus Christ, she refused the advances of the emperor Maxentius, who had her beheaded after her touch shattered the instrument meant to torture her.

parts_sanitized: Dioscorus was struck by lightning for beheading which saint, who thus became the patron saint of explosives and artillerymen, among other things?
Saint Barbara was venerated as one of the Fourteen Holy Helpers, another member of whom was this patron saint of England. He is well-known for slaying a dragon.

Another of the Fourteen Holy Helpers is this saint, who protected against sudden death. Since she consecrated her virginity to Jesus Christ, she refused the advances of the emperor Maxentius, who had her beheaded after her touch shattered the instrument meant to torture her.

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name these saints whose crazy life stories were popularized by the hagiographies of Jacobus de Voragine's Golden Legend.
parts: The Golden Legend claims that this patron saint of hunters and firefighters was once a Roman general who converted upon seeing a crucifix growing between a stag's antlers. Once he returned to Rome, he refused to make a pagan sacrifice and was condemned by Trajan to be burned alive inside a brass bull.

This saint and member of the Holy Helpers somehow, according to the Golden Legend, managed to pray her way through the stone walls of a fortified tower that her father had imprisoned him in. Because her father was punished for beheading her by being struck by lightning, she became the patron saint of explosives and artillerymen.

The Golden Legend states that this woman atoned for her sins in a desert near Provence where angels lifted her up every hour to feed her, and that she washed Jesus's feet by crying penitent tears over them, both of which outdo the boring suggestion that she conceived Christ's child.
answers: Mary Magdalene [or Mary of Magdala; or The Magdalene; prompt on Mary]
answers_sanitized: Mary Magdalene [or Mary of Magdala; or The Magdalene; prompt on Mary]

bonuses.leadin: This ethnicity has believed in a life force called ashé, which stems from their supreme deity, Olodumare. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this West African ethnicity whose pre-Christian religion included a divination ritual using palm nuts and the drum-accompanied "mounting" ritual, in which spirits called orishas could possess a person.
On this island, slaves of Yoruba descent blended their beliefs with Spanish Catholicism to create Santería.

In Cuba, this female saint and martyr is syncretized with Changó, the male fire and lightning orisha. This patron saint of artillery has a feast on December 4. Her father was struck by lightning for beheading her.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This ethnicity has believed in a life force called ashe, which stems from their supreme deity, Olodumare. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this West African ethnicity whose pre-Christian religion included a divination ritual using palm nuts and the drum-accompanied "mounting" ritual, in which spirits called orishas could possess a person.
On this island, slaves of Yoruba descent blended their beliefs with Spanish Catholicism to create Santeria.

In Cuba, this female saint and martyr is syncretized with Chango, the male fire and lightning orisha. This patron saint of artillery has a feast on December 4. Her father was struck by lightning for beheading her.

bonuses.leadin: One of these beings named Babalu Aye is connected to disease and healing, and one named Eshu represents death. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these manifestations of the Yoruban deity Olorun in a syncretic religion.
Orishas are spirits from this religion practiced in West Africa and the Caribbean. Some of its practitioners controversially sacrifice chickens.
The Orisha Shango is often conflated with this Catholic saint. She was tortured and killed by her father Dioscurus, who was subsequently struck by lightning.
